adr_entry:
## ADR-032: Add Accessibility Support **Status:** Accepted **Date:** 2026-04-24 **Ticket:** US-032 ### Context The application has accumulated seven dashboard modules, three action buttons, a responsive shell, and comprehensive reduced-motion CSS support across all animated components. However, several accessibility features required by CLAUDE.md are missing or incomplete: no skip-to-main navigation link, missing ARIA labels on the rerun button and module sections, no global `:focus-visible` rule for non-button interactive elements, no `role`/`aria-live` attributes on the intro overlay, and unverified color contrast ratios for key token pairs. The `src/ui/copy-button.ts` and `src/ui/share-image-button.ts` already have `aria-label` attributes. The fingerprint receipt already has `aria-live="polite"` on its dynamic content container. The `src/styles/button.css` already defines `.btn:focus-visible` with a 2px solid accent outline. Existing `tests/us-033/reduced-motion.test.ts` provides comprehensive reduced-motion verification. ### Decision Implement accessibility improvements across the shell, UI components, module sections, intro overlay, and global styles: 1. **Skip-to-main navigation link** in `src/layout/shell.ts` with visually-hidden styles in `src/styles/global.css` 2. **ARIA labels on buttons** — add `aria-label` to rerun button, add `aria-busy`/`aria-disabled` toggling during async operations on all three action buttons 3. **Global `:focus-visible` rule** for `a, button, [tabindex]` in `src/styles/global.css` — must not conflict with existing `.btn:focus-visible` in `button.css` 4. **Color contrast audit** of token pairs against WCAG AA (4.5:1 normal text, 3:1 large text) — key pairs: `--color-text-muted` (#6b7280) on `--color-bg` (#ffffff), `--color-accent` (#4a7c6f) on white, severity badge colors on backgrounds 5. **ARIA landmarks and labels on sections** — add `aria-label` to section elements in all five module renderers 6. **Intro overlay accessibility** — add `role="status"` and `aria-live="polite"` to intro overlay, ensure overlay DOM removal or `aria-hidden` on dismiss 7. **Keyboard navigation flow verification** — verify tab order follows visual layout, ensure no focus traps, consider `inert` attribute on shell during intro overlay 8. **Accessibility unit tests** in `tests/us-032/` 9. **Lint, typecheck, and test verification** 10. **Manual keyboard walkthrough** ### Architectural Impact Assessment **Scope:** Small — modifies approximately 8-10 existing files across `src/layout/`, `src/ui/`, `src/modules/`, and `src/styles/`, adds one new test directory (`tests/us-032/`). No new dependencies. No build pipeline changes. No configuration changes. **New dependencies:** None. All changes use standard DOM attributes (`aria-label`, `aria-live`, `aria-busy`, `role`, `inert`), CSS pseudo-classes (`:focus-visible`), and existing Vitest test infrastructure. No runtime or dev dependencies are added. **Build pipeline change:** None. Existing `npm run lint`, `npm run typecheck`, and `npm run test` scripts cover all modified and new files without modification. **Files modified:** - `src/layout/shell.ts` — add skip link as first child, minor DOM addition - `src/ui/rerun-button.ts` — add `aria-label='Re-run audit'`, toggle `aria-busy`/`aria-disabled` during async - `src/ui/copy-button.ts` — add `aria-busy`/`aria-disabled` toggling (already has `aria-label`) - `src/ui/share-image-button.ts` — add `aria-busy`/`aria-disabled` toggling (already has `aria-label`) - `src/modules/intro/intro-sequence.ts` — add `role="status"`, `aria-live="polite"` to overlay - `src/modules/zero-click-osint/zero-click-osint-module.ts` — add `aria-label` to section - `src/modules/shadow-profile/shadow-profile-module.ts` — add `aria-label` to section - `src/modules/threat-model/threat-model-module.ts` — add `aria-label` to section - `src/modules/hardening/hardening-module.ts` — add `aria-label` to section - `src/modules/permission-debt/permission-debt-module.ts` — add `aria-label` to section wrapper - `src/styles/global.css` — add skip-link styles and global `:focus-visible` rule - `src/styles/tokens.css` — potential minor color value adjustments if contrast audit fails **Risks identified and mitigations:** - **Color contrast adjustments may shift visual design:** The key concern is `--color-text-muted` (#6b7280) on `--color-bg` (#ffffff) — this pair yields approximately 4.6:1 contrast ratio, which passes WCAG AA for normal text (4.5:1 threshold) but is borderline. `--color-accent` (#4a7c6f) on white yields approximately 4.8:1, also passing AA. `--color-warning` (#eab308) on white yields approximately 2.8:1, which fails AA for normal text — this token is used as a badge background color (in OSINT cards, threat model cards, permission debt) where it likely pairs with dark text. The implementation should verify that warning badges use dark text (`--color-text`) rather than white text on the yellow background. If any token pair fails, adjustments should be minimal (e.g., darkening `--color-text-muted` from #6b7280 to #636b77 to increase contrast) while preserving the Stripe + threat intelligence lab aesthetic. Risk is low — the existing palette was chosen with professional design intent. - **Global `:focus-visible` specificity conflict with `.btn:focus-visible`:** The existing `.btn:focus-visible` rule in `button.css` has specificity `0,1,0` (one class), while a global `button:focus-visible` rule would have specificity `0,0,1` (one element). The `.btn` rule takes precedence, so no conflict. If the global rule uses `*:focus-visible` or `:focus-visible` (pseudo-class on all elements), it has specificity `0,0,1`, still lower than `.btn:focus-visible`. No conflict exists regardless of implementation approach. - **`inert` attribute browser support:** The `inert` attribute is supported in Chrome 102+, Firefox 112+, Safari 15.5+. For the project's target audience (privacy-conscious users likely running modern browsers), coverage is excellent. The plan correctly identifies the need to check compatibility. If a polyfill is needed, the `wicg-inert` polyfill is a lightweight option (~1KB), but given the target audience, native support should be sufficient. Fallback behavior (no `inert`) means the intro overlay does not trap focus but the overlay auto-removes after ~2 seconds, so the window of accessibility impact is very brief. - **`aria-label` on sections duplicates visible h2 text:** The plan's risk section correctly identifies this. Using `aria-labelledby` referencing the h2's `id` is the preferred pattern — it avoids content duplication and stays synchronized if heading text changes. The implementation should use `aria-labelledby` with id references (e.g., `

Shadow Profile

` + `
`) rather than `aria-label="Shadow Profile"`. This is a best-practice refinement, not an architectural concern. - **Intro overlay `role="status"` and `aria-live="polite"` interaction:** Adding `role="status"` implicitly sets `aria-live="polite"`, so explicitly adding `aria-live="polite"` is redundant but harmless. Screen readers will announce the overlay content when it appears. The overlay is removed from DOM after completion (verified in `intro-sequence.ts`), so no `aria-hidden` cleanup is needed — DOM removal is the cleanest approach and already implemented. - **Existing reduced-motion tests (US-033) must continue passing:** The plan's intro overlay changes (adding `role`/`aria-live` attributes) do not affect animation timing, CSS transitions, or DOM visibility. The `tests/us-033/reduced-motion.test.ts` suite tests CSS media queries, JS timing, and content visibility — none of these are affected by ARIA attribute additions. **No regression risk** to US-033 tests. - **Existing module tests must continue passing:** Adding `aria-label` or `aria-labelledby` attributes to section elements does not affect existing test selectors (which query by class name, tag name, or `.receipt-row`). Adding skip link to shell does not affect US-004 shell tests (which assert on header/main/footer existence and DOM order — the skip link is an `` element, not a landmark). US-031 footer tests assert on trust statement presence — unaffected. **No test regressions expected.** - **Keyboard navigation flow depends on DOM insertion order:** The current `main.ts` appends modules sequentially (zero-click → permission-debt → shadow-profile → threat-model → hardening), which creates a logical tab order matching visual order. The skip link targets `#dashboard` (the `
` element), allowing keyboard users to bypass the header. This is correct. The intro overlay renders before the shell and is removed from DOM after completion, so it does not interfere with post-intro keyboard navigation. - **Test scope — no axe-core or automated WCAG audit tool:** The plan uses Vitest unit tests for structural assertions (skip link exists, aria-labels present, focus-visible CSS defined, landmark structure correct). It does not introduce `axe-core` or similar automated WCAG audit tools. This is appropriate — `axe-core` is a substantial dependency (~500KB) and its integration with jsdom/happy-dom is limited. The structural tests combined with manual keyboard walkthrough and browser DevTools Lighthouse audit (recommended in testing plan) provide adequate coverage. **Consistency with existing architecture:** Fully consistent. All changes are attribute additions to existing DOM elements or CSS rule additions to existing stylesheets. No new components, no new modules, no new patterns. The skip link follows standard web accessibility patterns. ARIA attributes follow WAI-ARIA 1.2 specification. The `:focus-visible` pseudo-class is the modern standard for keyboard-only focus indicators. Tests follow the `tests/us-{number}/` convention. No runtime dependencies added. All changes are client-side per trust rules. The accessibility improvements directly fulfill CLAUDE.md's Accessibility Requirements section (keyboard accessible, focus states visible, sufficient contrast, reduced motion support, semantic headings and landmarks). **No breaking changes** to existing code, tests, build pipeline, or deployment. All modifications are additive attribute/style additions to existing elements. ### Consequences - **Positive:** Fulfills all four US-032 acceptance criteria (keyboard navigation, visible focus states, semantic landmarks, color contrast) and satisfies CLAUDE.md's Accessibility Requirements section in full - **Positive:** Skip-to-main link provides keyboard users immediate access to dashboard content, bypassing the header - **Positive:** Global `:focus-visible` rule ensures all interactive elements (not just buttons) have visible keyboard focus indicators - **Positive:** `aria-labelledby` on sections (preferred over `aria-label`) keeps landmark labels synchronized with visible heading text - **Positive:** Intro overlay `role="status"` ensures screen readers announce the opening sequence content - **Positive:** `aria-busy`/`aria-disabled` on action buttons provides screen reader context during async operations (copy, share, rerun) - **Positive:** Zero new dependencies keeps the bundle lightweight per CLAUDE.md constraints - **Positive:** All changes are client-side; no trust rule violations - **Positive:** No regression risk to existing US-033 reduced-motion tests or any other existing test suite - **Negative:** Color contrast adjustments (if needed) may subtly shift visual appearance — mitigated by minimal adjustments that preserve the design direction; `--color-text-muted` and `--color-accent` already pass WCAG AA - **Negative:** `inert` attribute on shell during intro overlay requires modern browser support (Chrome 102+, Firefox 112+, Safari 15.5+) — acceptable for the privacy-conscious target audience; brief 2-second overlay duration limits impact of fallback behavior - **Trade-off:** Using `aria-labelledby` with id references requires adding `id` attributes to all section headings, creating more DOM identifiers — this is the correct accessibility pattern and the identifiers serve a clear purpose
